moduleDescription: This module implements various synchronized I/O operations.

entries.name: File
entries.code: File = ptr CFile
entries.description: The type representing a file handle.

entries.name: FileMode
entries.code:
FileMode = enum
  fmRead,                   ## Open the file for read access only.
                             ## If the file does not exist, it will not
                             ## be created.
  fmWrite,                  ## Open the file for write access only.
                             ## If the file does not exist, it will be
                             ## created. Existing files will be cleared!
  fmReadWrite,              ## Open the file for read and write access.
                             ## If the file does not exist, it will be
                             ## created. Existing files will be cleared!
  fmReadWriteExisting,      ## Open the file for read and write access.
                             ## If the file does not exist, it will not be
                             ## created. The existing file will not be cleared.
  fmAppend                   ## Open the file for writing only; append data
                             ## at the end. If the file does not exist, it
                             ## will be created.
entries.description: The file mode when opening a file.

entries.name: FileSeekPos
entries.code:
FileSeekPos = enum
  fspSet,                   ## Seek to absolute value
  fspCur,                   ## Seek relative to current position
  fspEnd                     ## Seek relative to end
entries.description: Position relative to which seek should happen.

entries.name: FileHandle
entries.code: FileHandle = cint
entries.description: The type that represents an OS file handle; this is useful for low-level file access.

entries.name: stdin
entries.code: stdin {.importc: "__stdinp", header: "<stdio.h>".}: File
entries.description: The standard input stream.

entries.name: stdout
entries.code: stdout {.importc: "__stdoutp", header: "<stdio.h>".}: File
entries.description: The standard output stream.

entries.name: stderr
entries.code: stderr {.importc: "__stderrp", header: "<stdio.h>".}: File
entries.description: The standard error stream.

entries.name: stdmsg
entries.code: template stdmsg(): File
entries.description: Template which expands to either stdout or stderr depending on useStdoutAsStdmsg compile-time switch.

entries.name: readBuffer
entries.code:
proc readBuffer(f: File; buffer: pointer; len: Natural): int {.
    tags: [ReadIOEffect], gcsafe, raises: [IOError], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Reads len bytes into the buffer pointed to by buffer. Returns the actual number of bytes that have been read which may be less than len (if not as many bytes are remaining), but not greater.

entries.name: readBytes
entries.code:
proc readBytes(f: File; a: var openArray[int8 | uint8]; start, len: Natural): int {.
    tags: [ReadIOEffect], gcsafe.}
entries.description: Reads len bytes into the buffer a starting at a[start]. Returns the actual number of bytes that have been read which may be less than len (if not as many bytes are remaining), but not greater.

entries.name: readChars
entries.code:
proc readChars(f: File; a: var openArray[char]): int {.tags: [ReadIOEffect],
    gcsafe, raises: [IOError], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Reads up to a.len bytes into the buffer a. Returns the actual number of bytes that have been read which may be less than a.len (if not as many bytes are remaining), but not greater.

entries.name: readChars

pragmas: deprecated: "use other `readChars` overload, possibly via: readChars(toOpenArray(buf, start, len-1))"

entries.description: Reads len bytes into the buffer a starting at a[start]. Returns the actual number of bytes that have been read which may be less than len (if not as many bytes are remaining), but not greater.

entries.name: write
entries.code:
proc write(f: File; c: cstring) {.tags: [WriteIOEffect], gcsafe,
                                  raises: [IOError], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Writes a value to the file f. May throw an IO exception.

entries.name: writeBuffer
entries.code:
proc writeBuffer(f: File; buffer: pointer; len: Natural): int {.
    tags: [WriteIOEffect], gcsafe, raises: [IOError], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Writes the bytes of buffer pointed to by the parameter buffer to the file f. Returns the number of actual written bytes, which may be less than len in case of an error.

entries.name: writeBytes
entries.code:
proc writeBytes(f: File; a: openArray[int8 | uint8]; start, len: Natural): int {.
    tags: [WriteIOEffect], gcsafe.}
entries.description: Writes the bytes of a[start..start+len-1] to the file f. Returns the number of actual written bytes, which may be less than len in case of an error.

entries.name: writeChars
entries.code:
proc writeChars(f: File; a: openArray[char]; start, len: Natural): int {.
    tags: [WriteIOEffect], gcsafe, raises: [IOError], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Writes the bytes of a[start..start+len-1] to the file f. Returns the number of actual written bytes, which may be less than len in case of an error.

entries.name: write
entries.code:
proc write(f: File; s: string) {.tags: [WriteIOEffect], gcsafe,
                                 raises: [IOError], forbids: [].}

entries.name: close
entries.code: proc close(f: File) {.tags: [], gcsafe, sideEffect, raises: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Closes the file.

entries.name: readChar
entries.code:
proc readChar(f: File): char {.tags: [ReadIOEffect],
                               raises: [IOError, EOFError], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Reads a single character from the stream f. Should not be used in performance sensitive code.

entries.name: flushFile
entries.code: proc flushFile(f: File) {.tags: [WriteIOEffect], raises: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Flushes f's buffer.

entries.name: getFileHandle
entries.code: proc getFileHandle(f: File): FileHandle {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns the file handle of the file f. This is only useful for platform specific programming. Note that on Windows this doesn't return the Windows-specific handle, but the C library's notion of a handle, whatever that means. Use getOsFileHandle instead.

entries.name: getOsFileHandle
entries.code: proc getOsFileHandle(f: File): FileHandle {.raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns the OS file handle of the file f. This is only useful for platform specific programming.

entries.name: setInheritable
entries.code:
proc setInheritable(f: FileHandle; inheritable: bool): bool {.raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Controls whether a file handle can be inherited by child processes. Returns true on success. This requires the OS file handle, which can be retrieved via getOsFileHandle.

This procedure is not guaranteed to be available for all platforms. Test for availability with declared().

entries.name: readLine
entries.code:
proc readLine(f: File; line: var string): bool {.tags: [ReadIOEffect], gcsafe,
    raises: [IOError], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Reads a line of text from the file f into line. May throw an IO exception. A line of text may be delimited by LF or CRLF. The newline character(s) are not part of the returned string. Returns false if the end of the file has been reached, true otherwise. If false is returned line contains no new data.

entries.name: readLine
entries.code:
proc readLine(f: File): string {.tags: [ReadIOEffect], gcsafe,
                                 raises: [IOError, EOFError], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Reads a line of text from the file f. May throw an IO exception. A line of text may be delimited by LF or CRLF. The newline character(s) are not part of the returned string.

entries.name: write
entries.code:
proc write(f: File; i: int) {.tags: [WriteIOEffect], gcsafe, raises: [IOError],
                              forbids: [].}

entries.name: write
entries.code:
proc write(f: File; i: BiggestInt) {.tags: [WriteIOEffect], gcsafe,
                                     raises: [IOError], forbids: [].}

entries.name: write
entries.code:
proc write(f: File; b: bool) {.tags: [WriteIOEffect], gcsafe, raises: [IOError],
                               forbids: [].}

entries.name: write
entries.code:
proc write(f: File; r: float32) {.tags: [WriteIOEffect], gcsafe,
                                  raises: [IOError], forbids: [].}

entries.name: write
entries.code:
proc write(f: File; r: BiggestFloat) {.tags: [WriteIOEffect], gcsafe,
                                       raises: [IOError], forbids: [].}

entries.name: write
entries.code:
proc write(f: File; c: char) {.tags: [WriteIOEffect], gcsafe, raises: [],
                               forbids: [].}

entries.name: write
entries.code:
proc write(f: File; a: varargs[string, `$`]) {.tags: [WriteIOEffect], gcsafe,
    raises: [IOError], forbids: [].}

entries.name: endOfFile
entries.code: proc endOfFile(f: File): bool {.tags: [], gcsafe, raises: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Returns true if f is at the end.

entries.name: readAll
entries.code:
proc readAll(file: File): string {.tags: [ReadIOEffect], gcsafe,
                                   raises: [IOError], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Reads all data from the stream file.

Raises an IO exception in case of an error. It is an error if the current file position is not at the beginning of the file.

entries.name: writeLine
entries.code:
proc writeLine[Ty](f: File; x: varargs[Ty, `$`]) {.inline,
    tags: [WriteIOEffect], gcsafe.}
entries.description: Writes the values x to f and then writes "\n". May throw an IO exception.

entries.name: open
entries.code:
proc open(f: var File; filename: string; mode: FileMode = fmRead;
          bufSize: int = -1): bool {.tags: [], raises: [], gcsafe, forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Opens a file named filename with given mode.

Default mode is readonly. Returns true if the file could be opened. This throws no exception if the file could not be opened.

The file handle associated with the resulting File is not inheritable.

entries.name: reopen
entries.code:
proc reopen(f: File; filename: string; mode: FileMode = fmRead): bool {.
    tags: [], gcsafe, raises: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Reopens the file f with given filename and mode. This is often used to redirect the stdin, stdout or stderr file variables.

Default mode is readonly. Returns true if the file could be reopened.

The file handle associated with f won't be inheritable.

entries.name: open
entries.code:
proc open(f: var File; filehandle: FileHandle; mode: FileMode = fmRead): bool {.
    tags: [], raises: [], gcsafe, forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Creates a File from a filehandle with given mode.

Default mode is readonly. Returns true if the file could be opened.

The passed file handle will no longer be inheritable.

entries.name: open
entries.code:
proc open(filename: string; mode: FileMode = fmRead; bufSize: int = -1): File {.
    raises: [IOError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Opens a file named filename with given mode.

Default mode is readonly. Raises an IOError if the file could not be opened.

The file handle associated with the resulting File is not inheritable.

entries.name: setFilePos
entries.code:
proc setFilePos(f: File; pos: int64; relativeTo: FileSeekPos = fspSet) {.gcsafe,
    sideEffect, raises: [IOError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Sets the position of the file pointer that is used for read/write operations. The file's first byte has the index zero.

entries.name: getFilePos
entries.code:
proc getFilePos(f: File): int64 {.gcsafe, raises: [IOError], tags: [],
                                  forbids: [].}
entries.description: Retrieves the current position of the file pointer that is used to read from the file f. The file's first byte has the index zero.

entries.name: getFileSize
entries.code:
proc getFileSize(f: File): int64 {.tags: [ReadIOEffect], gcsafe,
                                   raises: [IOError], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Retrieves the file size (in bytes) of f.

entries.name: setStdIoUnbuffered
entries.code: proc setStdIoUnbuffered() {.tags: [], gcsafe, raises: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Configures stdin, stdout and stderr to be unbuffered.

entries.name: readFile
entries.code:
proc readFile(filename: string): string {.tags: [ReadIOEffect], gcsafe,
    raises: [IOError], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Opens a file named filename for reading, calls readAll and closes the file afterwards. Returns the string. Raises an IO exception in case of an error. If you need to call this inside a compile time macro you can use staticRead.

entries.name: writeFile
entries.code:
proc writeFile(filename, content: string) {.tags: [WriteIOEffect], gcsafe,
    raises: [IOError], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Opens a file named filename for writing. Then writes the content completely to the file and closes the file afterwards. Raises an IO exception in case of an error.

entries.name: writeFile
entries.code:
proc writeFile(filename: string; content: openArray[byte]) {.raises: [IOError],
    tags: [WriteIOEffect], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Opens a file named filename for writing. Then writes the content completely to the file and closes the file afterwards. Raises an IO exception in case of an error.

entries.name: readLines
entries.code:
proc readLines(filename: string; n: Natural): seq[string] {.
    raises: [IOError, EOFError], tags: [ReadIOEffect], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Reads n lines from the file named filename. Raises an IO exception in case of an error. Raises EOF if file does not contain at least n lines. Available at compile time. A line of text may be delimited by LF or CRLF. The newline character(s) are not part of the returned strings.

entries.name: readLines
entries.code:
template readLines(filename: string): seq[string] {.
    deprecated: "use readLines with two arguments".}

entries.name: lines
entries.code:
iterator lines(filename: string): string {.tags: [ReadIOEffect],
    raises: [IOError, IOError], forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Iterates over any line in the file named filename.

If the file does not exist IOError is raised. The trailing newline character(s) are removed from the iterated lines. Example:

entries.name: lines
entries.code:
iterator lines(f: File): string {.tags: [ReadIOEffect], raises: [IOError],
                                  forbids: [].}
entries.description:
Iterates over any line in the file f.

The trailing newline character(s) are removed from the iterated lines.

entries.name: `&=`
entries.code: template `&=`(f: File; x: typed)
entries.description: An alias for write.
